item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1649-A: [REDACTED] is many things. To us…"he", I suppose, is our sovereign leader. To you, he could be a whole new world.
Dr. Anborough: I've encountered several. Most of them aren't worth the investment.

I love that. It's such a Foundation thing to say.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
now designated SCP-███ and SCP-████

Why would two simultaneously acuired objects have their numbers different by one digit? And also, why are they described here? They are supposed to be either classified to the ''target audience'' of this document or mentioned as SCP-1649-2, SCP-1649-3, i guess.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's on the list of possibilities (it's a small list).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is SCP-1649-1 Bob?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmmm….
From the interview, it's clear that Group X have at least limited knowledge of the future. The flashlight produces a "space-time aperture", as opposed to a merely extradimensional one. Implying that something in the redacted details clearly indicates time manipulation effects. Heavily purpose-tailored biology in -A, and a rifle sufficiently advanced as to be indistinguishable from anomaly…
It all points to a cabal of time traveling fanatics, trying to ret-con Mr. Redacted into a significant historical figure.

I'm picturing the great-great-great grandson of Kim Jong-Il, no longer content with merely "correcting" the North Korean textbooks to reflect his awesome and totally-not-made-up-just-now accomplishments. If Wonderous Leader says he was at Woodstock, then by God everyone is going to believe that he was at Woodstock.

How far off base am I?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting. The subtle menace to it is pretty facinating. I feel like there could be some further suggestion as to what might have happened to Brian, or rather some further statement that something DID happen. Right now it requires heavy inferance.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He's not disinterested. He's uninterested, or (better) not interested. Disinterested implies that he's impartial or has no personal stake in what's being discussed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I really love this SCP, it's like a beggining of an invasion from the inside, I feel like if it lacks something, but it looks alright to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Subtlely menacing, I feel slightly disquieted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There was some broadcast that was this parody of right-wing talk radio, with aliens or an alternate universe or something. It got deleted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a really interesting concept. I think it could use perhaps a little more fleshing out, perhaps particularly related to the beliefs or actions of those affected by the broadcast. I'm also not sure that the additional SCP-classified items at the end of the article add to the story as a whole, though I don't know if you plan on going somewhere with them! If you're planning to add full SCP articles for those items, I wouldn't include their descriptions in this one.

I really like the mechanic of the Anboroughs, but, like Felgraf, I think that there could be something added there to make it stronger.

It's an interesting start, I'm curious where you want to take it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ SPOILER-ISH
– hide block

Charles is Brian's son and he's trying to figure out what happened regarding his father and this anomaly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What I find interesting is the swap between Charles and Brian Anborough. There's something in that, though I'm not sure what.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was more of the concept itself; I swear I've read about a talk show SCP somewhere else. Probably why I didn't feel anything when I reached the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. It's quite the amusing clusterfuck for the Foundation, what with Anborough's personal involvement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hell, I love the fact that you made it only have a 17% chance of working. Still gets it to carry all the same menacing implications of inserting this bizarre savior into history, but without giving the impression that mind control / perception-altering memetics are as easy to craft as a paper airplane, which is what it feels like when you see every other one on this site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … I don't know what this is, but I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To answer this comment thread, I was thinking about Tragedy of the Hanged King (SCP-701) while I was writing this, and Anaxagoras has suggested a connection to Zachary Callahan (see his post), though Callahan was not an influence on this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd say I third this, but I actually feel like I've written something like that interview before…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't much care for the dialogue as much; it reads more like movie dialogue than clinical interviewing. But I liked the SCP itself, so no vote overallm

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I also have that vague sense of having seen this before. Cannot tell you what or where, but at least you're not the only one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For some reason, I felt like I've seen this somewhere before while reading it. No vote for now, probably just me being dense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. Harmless at first glance, but even when you realize the implications the problem it causes isn't necessarily world-ending. But it's so..casual, with how it deals with itself and how it refers to events, and the implication of whatever is 'behind' it is rather chilling. My +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well it was meant to just be a test.

Zachary Callahan

Interesting idea; I'm okay with this interpretation (though it wasn't my original intent).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think I like this, but it feels incomplete. There's more to the story than this, and I hope you show it at some point. My biggest problem would be that a radio talk show sounds like an ineffective way to spread the effect, and there's not enough to support that this was just a test of a rollout.

Also, I choose to believe that 1649-1 is really Zachary Callahan.
